meta.name: RQ Relationship Questionnaire
meta.description: The Relationship Questionnaire (RQ) is a 4-item measure of adult attachment styles based on Bartholomew's four-category model. Each item describes a prototypical attachment pattern (Secure, Fearful, Preoccupied, Dismissing), and respondents rate how well each description matches their own relationship style.
meta.author: Bartholomew K, Horowitz LM (1991)
meta.reference: Bartholomew K, Horowitz LM. Attachment styles among young adults: a test of a four-category model. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1991;61(2):226-244.
meta.instruction: Following are four general relationship styles that people often report. Please read each description and rate how well it describes your typical approach to close relationships.

questions.text: It is easy for me to become emotionally close to others. I am comfortable depending on them and having them depend on me. I don't worry about being alone or having others not accept me.

I am uncomfortable getting close to others. I want emotionally close relationships, but I find it difficult to trust others completely, or to depend on them. I worry that I will be hurt if I allow myself to become too close to others.

I want to be completely emotionally intimate with others, but I often find that others are reluctant to get as close as I would like. I am uncomfortable being without close relationships, but I sometimes worry that others don't value me as much as I value them.
I am comfortable without close emotional relationships. It is very important to me to feel independent and self-sufficient, and I prefer not to depend on others or have others depend on me.

ranges.label: Low Overall Attachment Concern
ranges.description: Your overall attachment ratings are low across all styles. This may indicate you haven't strongly identified with any particular pattern. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Reflect on your relationship experiences to better understand your attachment tendencies
Consider discussing relationship patterns with a counselor
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Moderate Attachment Pattern
ranges.description: You show a moderate identification with one or more attachment styles. Review your subscale scores for more specific insights. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Review individual attachment style scores to understand your dominant pattern
Reflect on how your attachment style influences your relationships
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Strong Attachment Identification
ranges.description: You strongly identify with one or more attachment styles. Review your subscale profile for detailed insights. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Examine which specific attachment style(s) scored highest
Consider how these patterns affect your relationship satisfaction
If insecure patterns predominate, consider attachment-focused therapy
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

secure.label: Low Secure
secure.description: You do not strongly identify with the secure attachment style. You may experience some discomfort with closeness or trust. This indicates that your current sense of well-being and life satisfaction is relatively low. Focusing on self-care and lifestyle adjustments may be beneficial.
suggestions: Try doing one fulfilling activity each day
Stay connected with friends and family to build a sense of belonging
Explore new interests and activities
Practice a gratitude journal, focusing on positive aspects of life
If you consistently feel unmotivated, consider consulting a professional

secure.label: Moderate Secure
secure.description: You show some secure attachment characteristics. You are somewhat comfortable with closeness and interdependence. This level reflects a generally positive psychological state, where you can experience meaning and satisfaction in most aspects of life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive lifestyle
Share your experiences and positive feelings with others
Helping others is also an effective way to enhance well-being
Continue developing personal interests and social networks

secure.label: High Secure
secure.description: You strongly identify with the secure attachment style. You are comfortable with emotional closeness, trust, and mutual dependence. Your well-being and positive experiences are at a high level, reflecting strong psychological health and life adaptation.
suggestions: Keep up the habits and activities that bring you happiness
Share your positive experiences with others
Maintain gratitude and mindfulness practices
Focus on continued growth and deepening life meaning

fearful.label: Low Fearful
fearful.description: You do not strongly identify with the fearful attachment style. You are generally not overly worried about being hurt in relationships. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

fearful.label: Moderate Fearful
fearful.description: You show some fearful attachment characteristics. You may sometimes struggle between wanting closeness and fearing rejection. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

fearful.label: High Fearful
fearful.description: You strongly identify with the fearful attachment style. You desire close relationships but fear being hurt, making intimacy difficult. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

preoccupied.label: Low Preoccupied
preoccupied.description: You do not strongly identify with the preoccupied attachment style. You generally do not excessively worry about others' availability. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

preoccupied.label: Moderate Preoccupied
preoccupied.description: You show some preoccupied attachment characteristics. You may sometimes worry that others do not value you as much as you value them. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

preoccupied.label: High Preoccupied
preoccupied.description: You strongly identify with the preoccupied attachment style. You strongly desire closeness and may worry excessively about relationship security. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

dismissing.label: Low Dismissing
dismissing.description: You do not strongly identify with the dismissing attachment style. You are generally open to emotional closeness and interdependence. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

dismissing.label: Moderate Dismissing
dismissing.description: You show some dismissing attachment characteristics. You value independence but may still desire some close relationships. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

dismissing.label: High Dismissing
dismissing.description: You strongly identify with the dismissing attachment style. You highly value independence and self-sufficiency, preferring to avoid emotional dependence.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
